_comment: data/pentecostarion/pentecostarion-bright-week.json — Horologion v5.2
PURPOSE: Pentecostarion text overlay for the Bright Week period (Pascha through
Thomas Saturday). Provides stichera and aposticha texts for Vespers
during Bright Week, where the ordinary Octoechos is not used.
SCOPE (v5.2):
stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried — Bright Week Vespers
aposticha — Bright Week Vespers
The troparion-or-apolytikion slot is already resolved from
data/horologion/vespers-troparion.json (paschal_troparion, 'Christ is risen')
and requires no addition here.
The kathisma-reading slot correctly emits a liturgical omission rubric
(no kathisma during Bright Week — Paschal Canon replaces Psalter).
No text is needed here.
The theotokion-dismissal slot correctly emits a liturgical omission rubric
(Paschal Troparion itself serves as the dismissal during Bright Week).
No text is needed here.
KEY STRUCTURE:
Top-level key: 'bright_week'
Inner key: slot name ('stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried' | 'aposticha')
Each slot entry has type, label, text, source, resolvedAs.
LITURGICAL NOTE:
During Bright Week (Pascha Sunday through Thomas Saturday), the stichera
at 'Lord, I have cried' and the aposticha at Vespers are the Paschal
stichera from the Pentecostarion. The same fixed stichera are appointed
for all seven days of Bright Week — they do not vary by weekday.
Source: Pentecostarion (Jordanville 2005 / Hapgood tradition).
ARCHITECTURAL NOTE:
This file is deliberately separate from:
data/triodion/triodion-lenten-weekday.json (Great Lent weekdays)
data/triodion/holy-week/holy-week.json (Holy Week)
Bright Week belongs to the Pentecostarion, not the Triodion.
The engine detects Bright Week via toneResult.brightWeek === true,
which is a structurally distinct path from all Triodion branches.
COVERAGE NOTE (v5.2):
stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried: populated — fixed Bright Week Paschal stichera
aposticha: populated — fixed Bright Week Paschal aposticha
All other Bright Week slots: correctly handled by existing engine logic
(troparion already resolved; kathisma and theotokion correctly omitted)

_meta.coverage: Bright Week (Pascha Sunday through Thomas Saturday) — stichera and aposticha slots
_meta.note: v5.2 representative but partial. Stichera and aposticha populated for all Bright Week days (same fixed text). Other Bright Week slots handled by existing engine.

stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried.label: Paschal Stichera at "Lord, I have cried" — Bright Week
stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried.text:
Having beheld the Resurrection of Christ, let us worship the holy Lord Jesus, the only sinless One. We venerate Thy Cross, O Christ, and Thy holy Resurrection we praise and glorify; for Thou art our God, and we know no other beside Thee; we call on Thy name. Come, all ye faithful, let us venerate Christ's holy Resurrection; for, lo, through the Cross joy hath come to all the world. Ever blessing the Lord, we praise His Resurrection; for, enduring the Cross for us, He hath destroyed death by death.

Shine, shine, O new Jerusalem, for the glory of the Lord hath risen upon thee. Exult now and be glad, O Zion; and thou, pure Theotokos, rejoice in the Resurrection of thy Son.
stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried.note: These Paschal stichera are appointed at 'Lord, I have cried' throughout Bright Week Vespers. The same text is used on all seven days.

aposticha.label: Paschal Aposticha — Bright Week
aposticha.text:
Christ is risen from the dead, trampling down death by death, and upon those in the tombs bestowing life.

It is the Day of Resurrection. Let us be radiant, O ye peoples. Pascha! The Pascha of the Lord! For from death unto life and from earth unto heaven hath Christ God brought us over, as we sing the song of victory.

Let God arise, let His enemies be scattered; let those who hate Him flee from before His face. As smoke vanisheth, so let them vanish; as wax melteth before the fire, so let the ungodly perish before the face of those who love God; and let the righteous be glad.

This is the day which the Lord hath made; let us rejoice and be glad therein. Pascha of beauty, the Pascha of the Lord. A Pascha all-venerable hath dawned for us. Pascha! With joy let us embrace one another. O Pascha! Ransom from sorrow! For today Christ hath shone forth from the tomb as from a bridal chamber, and hath filled the women with joy, saying: Proclaim it to the Apostles.
aposticha.note: These Paschal aposticha are appointed throughout Bright Week Vespers. The same text is used on all seven days.
